name: Papaya Seeds
description: Traditional anthelmintic and intestinal anti-parasitic agent. Carica papaya seeds contain active Benzyl isothiocyanate (BITC) compounds which exhibit powerful vermifuge activity, paralyzing helminths to promote rapid digestive evacuation.
preparation: Harvest seeds from a ripe papaya. Air-dry seeds in a shaded, well-ventilated area until dark and wrinkled. Grind the dry seeds into a fine powder. Mix 1 teaspoon (approx. 4g) of dried seed powder with 1 tablespoon of raw honey. Administer orally on an empty stomach in the morning for 3 consecutive days.
safetyAlert: Avoid large doses in pregnancy (theoretical embryotoxic risks from carpaine). May cause mild temporary stomach cramps.
interactions: Safe with standard anthelmintics. May enhance blood thinning; use caution if patient is on anticoagulants.
